_meta.decisions_authorised: D1 through D7 (D7A variant), all confirmed by researcher 2026-05-14
_meta.prerequisite: All actions in groups 1-4 require regeneration of M46 comprehensive report after completion. Action group 5 (Session A extraction) is a separate programme-level pipeline task.
_meta.execution_order: Groups must be executed in order 1 → 2 → 3 → 4. Group 5 is independent and can be queued separately.

action_group_1.title: T2 → M46: Move 8 terms from T2 Supplementary to M46
action_group_1.rationale: D2 confirmed. These terms carry wealth/richness/abundance vocabulary that belongs in the reconstituted M46 domain cluster. They are currently in T2 as supporting terms without characteristic assignment.
action_group_1.impact_on_T2: 8 terms removed from T2. T2 gloss field updated to remove these entries.
action_group_1.impact_on_M46: 8 terms added to M46. M46 term count increases from 5 to 13.

cc_operations.note: SHA.MAN root adjective: fat, rich, fertile. Neh 9:25 co-occurs with a.dan and sha.man. Key wealth-domain adjective.
SHA.MAN root verb: to become fat/rich. Deu 32:15 Jeshurun grew fat and kicked — prototypical prosperity → apostasy sequence. Neh 9:25.
SHA.MAN root abstract noun: fatness, rich things. Isa 17:4; Dan 11:24; Gen 27:28,39. Names material richness as a condition.
Greek adjective: self-sufficient, content. Phil 4:11 root form. The inner posture of sufficiency regardless of material circumstance. Pairs with autarkeia (G841, shared from M15).
EDEN root verb: to live in luxury, take delight in abundance. Neh 9:25 — ate, were filled, grew fat, and delighted (a.dan) in God's goodness. Inner engagement with material abundance.

SHA.A.NAN root: at ease, complacent, careless — specifically the complacency of the materially secure. Isa 32:9,11; Amos 6:1 ('woe to those who are at ease in Zion'); Jer 49:31; Zec 1:15. VERIFY: CC to confirm Strong's H7961 matches T2 mti record with translit shal.a.nan before executing.
CHOSEN root verb: to store up, hoard, lay in store. Same root family as H2633 cho.sen (wealth, already in M46). Isa 23:18; Pro 2:7. Names the inner act of accumulation and hoarding.

Greek adjective: oily, rich, luxurious. Rev 18:14 only — the luxurious goods of Babylon lost in judgment. NT wealth-domain vocabulary. Low occurrence but thematically central to M46's apocalyptic register.

action_group_2.title: M46 → M22: Move H3520B ke.vud.dah (mti_id=1870)
action_group_2.rationale: D6 confirmed. ke.vud.dah is extracted_thin (1 occurrence, 2 verses), origin registry R015 boastfulness (C09), and its KAVED root (glory/honour/weight) belongs in M22 Praise/Thanksgiving/Glory rather than M46 Abundance.
action_group_2.impact_on_M46: 1 term removed (ke.vud.dah). M46 term count: 13 → 12. VCG 3422 (1870-001) migrates to M22.
action_group_2.impact_on_M22: 1 term added (ke.vud.dah, mti_id=1870). 1 VCG added (3422, 1870-001). 2 verses added (Eze 23:41 vc_id=143931, Psa 45:13 vc_id=143932). M22 status unchanged (Not started — safe to add terms).

cc_operations.note: KAVED root (ka.vod, glory/honour/weight family). Material richness or splendour as the setting in which inner spiritual orientation is enacted. 2 verses: Eze 23:41 (stately couch — idolatrous context), Psa 45:13 (glorious princess — royal splendour). Extracted_thin; origin R015 boastfulness (C09).

cc_operations.vcg_description: Term names material richness or splendour as the setting in which inner spiritual orientation — faithfulness or its corruption toward God — is enacted or exposed

action_group_3.title: M46 internal: Move H1878 da.shen group 111-002 to M39 (D7A)
action_group_3.rationale: D7A confirmed. Group 111-002 (VCG id=51) carries anointing vocabulary (Psa 23:5) belonging to the M39 Blessing/Favour/Grace register. Moving 1 VCG and 1 verse to M39. H1878 da.shen itself stays in M46 (group 111-001 retained). M39 impact: +1 VCG, +1 verse. No M39 findings disrupted.
action_group_3.impact_on_M46: VCG 51 (111-002) migrates out. H1878 da.shen retains group 111-001 in M46. 1 verse (Psa 23:5) removed from M46 verse pool.
action_group_3.impact_on_M39: 1 VCG added (111-002). 1 verse added (Psa 23:5). No existing findings disrupted. M39 Analysis Completed status: add addendum note only.

cc_operations.vcg_description: Term names the anointing that signals divine presence and favour — the saturation of the head with oil as the emblem of divine blessing upon the inner person
cc_operations.note: H1878 da.shen (mti_id=111) remains in M46 as the owner term. Only this VCG and its one verse migrate. The term is split across M46 (111-001, prosperity) and M39 (111-002, anointing) — this is a legitimate cross-cluster VCG split.

verses_in_group.verse_text: You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies; you anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows.

action_group_4.title: autarkeia (G841) shared term: Add M46 verse_context rows (D3)
action_group_4.rationale: D3 confirmed. autarkeia (G841) stays in M15 Wisdom (Analysis Completed — no M15 records modified). Two specific verses whose inner-being content sits in the wealth domain are additionally registered in M46 via new verse_context rows. This implements the shared-term mechanism: the same term appears in two clusters for different verse-level reasons.
action_group_4.impact_on_M46: 2 new verse_context rows added for autarkeia (G841) pointing to M46. Term appears in both M15 and M46.

cc_operations.note: DO NOT modify any M15 record. Add new verse_context rows for M46 only. CC to resolve mti_id for G841 from mti_terms table before executing.

verses_to_add_in_M46.rationale: Godliness with contentment (autarkeia) is great gain — the wealth-domain framing ('great gain') places this verse in M46; autarkeia names the inner posture of sufficiency that constitutes true wealth. Distinct from M15 dimension (wisdom/virtue).

Having all sufficiency (autarkeia) in all things so that you may abound in every good work — material sufficiency enabling generosity; the inner-being response to material provision. Distinct from M15 dimension (wisdom/sufficiency as virtue).

action_group_5.title: Session A extraction brief: 8 new terms for M46 (D5)
action_group_5.rationale: D5 confirmed. These terms were not excluded intentionally from the programme — they were simply not identified as relevant until now. They represent the primary NT and OT vocabulary for the state of being rich/poor and the condition of wealth, which is the core domain of the reconstituted M46. Session A extraction pipeline required before these terms can be placed in M46.
action_group_5.pipeline: Session A extraction (STEP Bible) → mti_terms registration → verse extraction → M46 assignment

priority_tier_1.note: HIGH priority — core vocabulary of the M46 wealth domain. Substantial occurrence counts. Theologically central.

terms.inner_being_significance: Names the state of being materially wealthy. Rev 3:17 Laodicean church ('I am rich') is a primary inner-being distortion case — self-assessed richness masking spiritual poverty. The rich young ruler (Luke 18:23) is a portrait of wealth as inner-being obstacle.

Names material and spiritual wealth as a quality. Notably used of divine riches (Rom 11:33 'riches of his wisdom and knowledge') — the term operates across material and spiritual registers. Jas 5:2 'your riches have rotted' — wealth as morally corruptible possession.

terms.gloss: to be rich, to become rich
terms.inner_being_significance: Luke 12:21 'not rich toward God' — the contrast between material wealth-accumulation and inner-being richness toward God. 1 Tim 6:9 'those who desire to be rich fall into temptation' — the desire to become rich as inner-being peril.

2 Cor 8:9 'he became poor so that you by his poverty might become rich' — the Christological wealth transfer; the richest inner-being statement about poverty and wealth in the NT. 2 Cor 6:10 'as poor yet making many rich' — the paradox of apostolic poverty producing inner wealth.

The counterpart to plousios — the rich/poor contrast pair. Matt 5:3 'poor in spirit' extends ptōchos into the inner-being register explicitly. Luke 4:18 'good news to the poor' — the poor as the primary recipients of the gospel. Jas 2:2-6 — the moral distortion of treating the poor with contempt. Essential to M46 as the domain of wealth also defines the domain of poverty.

priority_tier_2.note: MEDIUM priority — OT core vocabulary. High co-occurrence with existing M46 terms.

terms.inner_being_significance: Pro 8:18 already in M46 hon group — 'riches and honor are with me, enduring wealth (o.sher) and righteousness' — o.sher co-occurs directly with hon in Wisdom's speech. High cross-term co-occurrence with existing M46 terms. The primary OT noun for the state of material wealth.

The rich person as a social and moral category in Proverbs and Ecclesiastes. Pro 10:15 already in M46 hon group — 'a rich man's wealth is his strong city'; a.shir co-occurs with hon in that verse. Names the person defined by wealth and the inner-being dynamics that follow.

The verb of becoming rich. Hos 12:8 'Ephraim has said, Ah, but I am rich, I have found wealth for myself' — the inner self-declaration of wealth as spiritual delusion. Pro 23:4 'do not toil to acquire wealth; be discerning enough to desist' — the wisdom-shaped restraint on the drive to become rich.

action_group_5.extraction_note: CC to initiate Session A pipeline for all 8 terms. After extraction and verse registration, all terms are assigned to M46 as primary cluster. The extraction gap is NOT a programme error — these terms were simply not yet assessed. Standard Session A workflow applies.
